findings.eventstream_result: EMPTY - no SSE events delivered (identical to #311/#312/#313)
findings.response_body: Failed to load response data - empty body, no data frames written

keep_alive_analysis.expected_behavior: h2_mode=2 should allow APF to write SSE data frames before END_STREAM, unlike GOAWAY which kills the entire connection
keep_alive_analysis.actual_behavior: 200 OK returned with empty body, then stream closed. Zero SSE events in EventStream. Response tab shows 'Failed to load response data'
keep_alive_analysis.conclusion: The APF template response body is NOT being written to the H2 stream regardless of h2_mode. The issue is not GOAWAY vs keep-alive - it is that the template generates no response body content at all
keep_alive_analysis.root_cause_hypothesis: Template id=26 likely has empty or malformed response body. The SSE event data in the template may not be properly formatted or the template engine may not be injecting body content into the H2 DATA frames
keep_alive_analysis.recommendation: 1. Check template id=26 response body content in DB - verify SSE events are actually defined. 2. Check APF C++ code to confirm template body is written to H2 stream. 3. Consider using a completely different approach: return HTTP 4xx with JSON error body instead of trying to inject SSE events

four_test_summary.312: h2_mode=1 + removed Content-Length:0 → 0 SSE events → network error
four_test_summary.313: h2_mode=1 + h2_hold_request=1 → 0 SSE events → network error
four_test_summary.314: h2_mode=2 (keep-alive) + h2_hold_request=1 → 0 SSE events → network error

notes: 4th DeepSeek attempt. h2_mode=2 (keep-alive) also failed - zero SSE events, empty response body. The problem is NOT h2_mode (GOAWAY vs keep-alive) - the template is simply not generating any response body content. All 4 tests (#311-#314) show identical empty EventStream. Recommend checking template body content in DB or switching to HTTP 4xx error approach.
